children.heading: Cooperation with other agencies
children.label: a Cooperation with other agencies
children.text: The Commission may cooperate with any Department or agency of the Government, any State, territory, district, or possession, or department, agency, or political subdivision thereof, any foreign futures authority, any department or agency of a foreign government or political subdivision thereof, or any person.

children.heading: Employment of investigators, experts, Administrative Law Judges, consultants, clerks, and other personnel; contracts
children.label: b Employment of investigators, experts, Administrative Law Judges, consultants, clerks, and other personnel; contracts

children.text: The Commission shall have the authority to employ such investigators, special experts, Administrative Law Judges, clerks, and other employees as it may from time to time find necessary for the proper performance of its duties and as may be from time to time appropriated for by Congress.

The Commission may employ experts and consultants in accordance with , and compensate such persons at rates not in excess of the maximum daily rate prescribed for GS–18 under .section 3109 of title 5section 5332 of title 5

The Commission shall also have authority to make and enter into contracts with respect to all matters which in the judgment of the Commission are necessary and appropriate to effectuate the purposes and provisions of this chapter, including, but not limited to, the rental of necessary space at the seat of Government and elsewhere.

The Commission may request (in accordance with the procedures set forth in subchapter II of chapter 31 of title 5) and the Office of Personnel Management shall authorize pursuant to the request, eight positions in the Senior Executive Service in addition to the number of such positions authorized for the Commission on .October 28, 1992

children.heading: Expenses
children.label: c Expenses
children.text: All of the expenses of the Commissioners, including all necessary expenses for transportation incurred by them while on official business of the Commission, shall be allowed and paid on the presentation of itemized vouchers therefor approved by the Commission.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this chapter for each of the fiscal years 2008 through 2013.

children.heading: Relation to other law, departments, or agencies
children.label: e Relation to other law, departments, or agencies
A
children.text: criminal prosecution under any Federal criminal statute;

children.label: i
children.text: that is not conducted on or subject to the rules of a registered entity or exempt board of trade;

children.label: ii
children.text: (except as otherwise specified by the Commission by rule or regulation) that is not conducted on or subject to the rules of any board of trade, exchange, or market located outside the United States, its territories or possessions; or

children.label: iii
children.text: that is not subject to regulation by the Commission under section 6c or 23 of this title; or

children.label: C
children.text: the application of any Federal or State statute, including any rule or regulation thereunder, to any person required to be registered or designated under this chapter who shall fail or refuse to obtain such registration or designation.

children.label: A
children.text: an electronic trading facility excluded under section 2(e) of this title; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: an agreement, contract, or transaction that is excluded from this chapter under section 2(c) or 2(f) of this title or sections 27 to 27f of this title, or exempted under (regardless of whether any such agreement, contract, or transaction is otherwise subject to this chapter).section 6(c) of this title

children.heading: Investigative assistance to foreign futures authorities
children.label: f Investigative assistance to foreign futures authorities

children.text: On request from a foreign futures authority, the Commission may, in its discretion, provide assistance in accordance with this section if the requesting authority states that the requesting authority is conducting an investigation which it deems necessary to determine whether any person has violated, is violating, or is about to violate any laws, rules or regulations relating to futures or options matters that the requesting authority administers or enforces. The Commission may conduct such investigation as the Commission deems necessary to collect information and evidence pertinent to the request for assistance. Such assistance may be provided without regard to whether the facts stated in the request would also constitute a violation of the laws of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the requesting authority has agreed to provide reciprocal assistance to the Commission in futures and options matters; and

children.label: B
children.text: compliance with the request would prejudice the public interest of the United States.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Commission may accept payment and reimbursement, in cash or in kind, from a foreign futures authority, or made on behalf of such authority, for necessary expenses incurred by the Commission, its members, and employees in carrying out any investigation, or in providing any other assistance to a foreign futures authority, pursuant to this section. Any payment or reimbursement accepted shall be considered a reimbursement to the appropriated funds of the Commission.

children.heading: Computerized futures trading
children.label: g Computerized futures trading
children.text: Consistent with its responsibilities under , the Commission is directed to facilitate the development and operation of computerized trading as an adjunct to the open outcry auction system. The Commission is further directed to cooperate with the Office of the United States Trade Representative, the Department of the Treasury, the Department of Commerce, and the Department of State in order to remove any trade barriers that may be imposed by a foreign nation on the international use of electronic trading systems.section 22 of this title

children.heading: Regulation of swaps as insurance under State law
children.label: h Regulation of swaps as insurance under State law

children.text: shall not be considered to be insurance; and
may not be regulated as an insurance contract under the law of any State.
